uk: 5🕂 Преподобного й богоносного отця нашого Сави Освященного
saints.en: Ven. and God-Bearing Sabbas the Sanctified

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи взиваю» – 6 стихир прп., Слава: прп, І нині: богородичний-догмат 2-го гласу. Вхід. Прокімен дня і три читання прп: Муд. 3:1-9, Муд. 5:15-6:3, Прип. 2:1-9. Стиховня прп., зі своїми приспівами. Сл.: прп., І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом «Слава». Тропар прп., Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних за гласом тропаря прп. Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби прп.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар прп. (2), Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних, за гласом тропаря прп. Катизма чергова з сідальними прп., також полієлей з величаннями. Степенна 4-го гласу, перший антифон. Прокімен, Єв. – Лк. 24 зач.; 6, 17-23. Пс. 50, і стихира прп. Канон Богородиці і прп. «Христос народжується». По 3-ій пісні – сідальний прп.; по 6-ій пісні – кондак-ікос прп. По 9-ій – світильний прп. Хвалитних стихир прп. – 4, Слава: прп., І нині: богород. за гласом наславника. По великому славослов'ї з трисвятим – тропар прп., Слава, І нині: богород. з недільних за гласом тропаря прп. Відпуст великий з помин. тільки служби прп.
Часи: на всіх – тропар і кондак прп.
Літургія: все – тільки прп. Апостол і євангелія: Ряд.: Ап. – Еф. 218 зач.; 1, 16-23. Єв. – Лк. 67 зач.; 12, 32-40. Прп.: Ап. – Євр. 335 зач.; 13, 17-21. Єв. – Мт. 43 зач.; 11, 27-30.

troparion_en.text:
With the streams of your tears you cultivated the barrenness of the desert;  /  and by your sighs from the
depths, you bore fruit a hundredfold in your labors.  /  You became a luminary, shining upon the world with
miracles. /  O our righteous Father Savva, intercede with Christ God that our souls be saved.

kontakion_en.text:
(Podoben: “O Victorious Leader...”)
From your youth you were offered to God  as an unblemished1 sacrifice because of your virtue,  / having been dedicated to Him before your birth, O blessed Savva.  /  You were an adornment of monastics and a praiseworthy citizen of the desert.  / Therefore, we cry to you:  “Hail, greatly renowned Father.”

troparion.text:
With the streams of your tears you cultivated the barrenness of the desert;  /  and by your sighs from the
depths, you bore fruit a hundredfold in your labors.  /  You became a luminary, shining upon the world with
miracles. /  O our righteous Father Savva, intercede with Christ God that our souls be saved.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “O Victorious Leader...”)
From your youth you were offered to God  as an unblemished1 sacrifice because of your virtue,  / having been dedicated to Him before your birth, O blessed Savva.  /  You were an adornment of monastics and a praiseworthy citizen of the desert.  / Therefore, we cry to you:  “Hail, greatly renowned Father.”

troparion.text: Because of your virtuous and noble life, the most generous God raised you to the throne of the God-saved kingdom of Kazan  / and enlightened the Tatar people by your teachings, O Saint Gurίas.  / You brought many to Christ's chosen flock, and lived a godly life, giving aid to the poor; /  and after your glorification, your body, which lay hidden for many years, wondrously appeared from the depths of the earth, /  and your coffin was filled with fragrant myrrh for all those who come with faith to receive this myrrh at the shrine of your revered relics,  / which pour forth healing for all.  / O Holy Hierarch Gurίas, pray to Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

oca_saints.name: Martyr Anastasius the Fuller of Salona in Dalmatia

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Anastasius, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Anastasius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Karion (Cyrion) and his son, Venerable Zachariah, of Egypt

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy fathers Karion and Zachariah, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Nectarius of Bitolya and Mount Athos

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Nectarius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Philotheus of Karyes, Mount Athos

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Philotheus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: As venerable and proven ascetics, as honorable and renowned martyrs,  / and as models of the desert life, O most exalted Fathers, /  we sing hymns of praise to you,  / for you have trampled upon serpents.

troparion.text: Your lamb Crispina calls out to You, O Jesus, in a loud voice: / "I love You, my Bridegroom, and in seeking You I endure suffering. / In baptism I was crucified so that I might reign in You, / and I died so that I might live with You. / Accept me as a pure sacrifice, / for I have offered myself in love." / Through her prayers save our souls, since You are merciful.
